turns.text: Here's a 5-question quiz on PPO, DPO, and GRPO alignment methods — mix of MCQs and short answers covering the pipeline, key differences, and implementation details.

suggestions.title: PPO, DPO, GRPO Alignment Methods Quiz

artifact.title: AI623 Quiz: PPO, DPO, GRPO Alignment Methods

questions.text: In the LLM Alignment Pipeline, which component is trained using human preference data to score model outputs?

A researcher has collected pairwise preference data from human annotators but does not have the computational resources to train and maintain a separate reward model. Which alignment method would be most suitable for this scenario?
Which statement best explains why PPO requires more model components than either DPO or GRPO?
Explain the key difference between how GRPO and PPO handle advantage estimation without requiring the same set of supporting models.

Consider an LLM alignment scenario where computational efficiency is critical. Discuss how the use of LoRA and PEFT techniques could enable practical deployment of any of the three methods (PPO, DPO, GRPO). What aspect of the alignment pipeline does PEFT primarily optimize?

answer_key.answer: GRPO avoids the need for a critic model by sampling multiple outputs per prompt and normalizing rewards relative to the group statistics. Instead of learning a separate value function like PPO's critic, GRPO uses the relative ordering and reward distribution within the generated group to estimate ad

LoRA and PEFT techniques enable efficient implementation of all three methods by reducing the number of trainable parameters during fine-tuning. Rather than updating all weights in the full models (policy, reference, reward, critic), PEFT selectively updates a small subset of parameters (low-rank ad

turns.prompt: confirm which slides u used for this quiz
turns.text:
```
I grounded this quiz in the selected slides from 11_ppo_dpo_grpo_alignment.pptx:

• **Slide 3** — The LLM Alignment Pipeline (reward model, policy optimization, KL divergence)
• **Slide 4** — PPO specifics (separately trained reward model, policy/reference/reward/critic models, most complex)
• **Slide 5** — DPO and GRPO distinctions (DPO: no reward model; GRPO: no critic model, group sampling)
• **Slide 6** — Summary comparison (model requirements, LoRA/PEFT efficiency)

The quiz questions directly target the core differentiators: which models each method needs, why DPO bypasses the reward model, and how GRPO uses group normalization instead of a critic — all explicitly covered in those slides.
```
